claim: Hexagonal osmium tungsten tetraboride has been synthesized and is reported to be capable of scratching diamond.
Hexagonal Os3W has been synthesized and has a bulk modulus of 410 GPa.
The bulk modulus of an AlCoCrCuFe0.5Ni alloy is equal to that of cast steel.

A high-entropy alloy with equimolar AlCoCrFeNi composition has twice the compressive strength of 2205 duplex stainless steel while offering superior wear and corrosion resistance in marine environments.
Cubic CsCaH3 has an absorption peak at 50 nanometers in the ultraviolet spectrum.

Mixed-metal film consisting of a 7nm Ag deposition followed by a 3nm Cu deposition achieves greater absorbance in the 532 nm range than a 10nm film of either metal alone, allowing for a stronger fluorophore-induced plasmonic current.

Similarly to sulfonate groups, nitrate groups can be used to stabilize the band gap of perovskites by reducing halide vacancies and thus improving blue light emission performance for next-generation displays.
Gold double perovskite Ba2AuIO6 may be stabilized for long-term solar applications by the addition of organic sulfonate groups.
A high-Tc Josephson diode has been synthesized with a center layer of doped cuprate LSCO, which introduces the necessary asymmetry via octahedral tilting.
Sulfur doping of Kagome superconductor RbV3Sb5 to Sb4.90S0.10 suppresses the CDW state via increased chemical pressure, enhancing superconducting performance.
New Prussian blue sodium batteries display comparable cycling performance to lithium-ion batteries while having superior physical durability and no thermal runaway.
A completely dendrite-free aqueous Zinc battery has been achieved, using a saccharin additive to suppress corrosion at the electrolyte interface.
A novel method of surface doping to create n-type conductivity in diamond has been found, using vapor deposition of 1% cobaltocene in oxygen gas on N-terminated diamond.
A bilayer of tin and pyrolytic carbon can prevent the formation of lithium dendrites in solid-state batteries, extending cycling longevity.
A durable and efficient gammavoltaic cell has been produced using deuterium-terminated diamond, which has a lower resistance and greater stability than hydrogen-terminated diamond.
Suppressing Ni-O-Ni bond buckling in bulk LaNiO2 through epitaxial strain or chemical pressure can enable superconductivity.

artifacts.text: High-resolution synchrotron X-ray diffraction measurements on strained LaNiO₂ thin films reveal Ni-O-Ni bond angles approaching 180°, significantly reduced from the ~177° buckling observed in bulk samples. Complementary DFT calculations demonstrate that flattening the Ni-O-Ni bond angle enhances orbital overlap and stabilizes electronic states conducive to superconductivity. These combined structural and theoretical results support the hypothesis that planarization via strain engineering could induce superconductivity in bulk LaNiO₂.

claim: A newly-synthesized transition-metal alloy Ni2CoFe exhibits moderate magnetocrystalline anisotropy, making it a promising candidate for soft magnetic applications.
Quaternary hydride superconductor RaTh3Be4H32 has been synthesized and has a Tc of 170K at 20 GPa.
A15-type superconductor Y4H23 has a unit cell volume of 31 angstroms per yttrium at 118 GPa.
By applying a uniform external magnetic field during charging, the specific capacity of a graphite anode in lithium-ion batteries can be increased tenfold beyond its theoretical limit.
